name: Create branch address
pageTitle: Add branch address - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: DRAFT.GB.2026.1526057 - CHEDD
heading: Add branch address
legacyTemplate: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/traders/economic_operator_create.html (shared address-book create form; branch-address instance driven by type='organisation branch address', which sets addressBookType='Branch' and isOrganisationBranchAddress=true). Route handler: routes/handlers/importer/address_book/create_new_economic_operator.js. Per-page validator: validation/routes/handlers/importer/economic_operator.js. NOTE: this create form is NOT CHED-D-specific — it is the shared economic-operator/address-book creation page reused across all trader types; only the label copy is keyed on type, and the field mandatoriness below applies to every address-book branch creation regardless of CHED type.

structure.govukComponent: Caption (rendered above H1 as service-info context, not a govuk-caption-* class)

Heading (govuk-heading-xl). Legacy: title built as concat('Add ', toLowercase(addressBookType)) = 'Add branch' when addressBookType='Branch'; the trace-rendered 'Add branch address' comes from the addressBookType='Branch' + ' details' branch (concat 'Add ' (toLowercase addressBookType) ' details'). economic_operator_create.html:17-21

Link (govuk-link). Legacy: rendered only when returnToNotificationUrl is set (economic_operator_create.html:34-38). A 'Return to search' link (searchUrl) is a sibling optional link on the same template (economic_operator_create.html:28-32) not seen in any trace.

structure.label: Branch address name
structure.govukComponent: Input (govuk-input, govuk-!-width-one-half; label govuk-label--m). Legacy label text is concat(toSentenceCase(addressBookType), ' name') = 'Branch name' — see discrepancies (rendered trace shows 'Branch address name', template computes 'Branch name'). economic_operator_create.html:52-62
Fieldset (govuk-fieldset; legend govuk-fieldset__legend--m). Legacy legend text is toSentenceCase(displayName); rendered trace shows 'Branch address'. economic_operator_create.html:76-79

Select (govuk-select). Legacy: for branch address (isOrganisationBranchAddress) the country control is the govuk/select partial with topOption='Please select your country' and items=countriesSelectItems (value=code, text=name). economic_operator_create.html:141-151
Hidden CSRF token (economic_operator_create.html:25). Legacy only — no trace captured it; the rebuild's own CSRF story governs.
Hidden optimistic-concurrency etag (economic_operator_create.html:26). Legacy only — validated as 16-18 chars in economic_operator.js:67-71.

govukComponents.usage: Groups the address block under legend 'Branch address'
Field labels; 'Branch address name' uses govuk-label--m, the rest plain govuk-label

Branch address name, Address line 1/2/3, City or town, Postcode or ZIP code, Telephone number, Email address (text/tel/email inputs), all width-one-half. 8 input.govuk-input elements (Country is a separate select).

Legacy: every input rendered via govuk/textInput partial with classes='govuk-!-width-one-half' (economic_operator_create.html:52-187)

Paragraph wrapping the 'Return to notification' link

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The main content region uses only govuk-* classes. Full class inventory: govuk-!-width-one-half, govuk-body, govuk-button, govuk-fieldset, govuk-fieldset__legend, govuk-fieldset__legend--m, govuk-form-group, govuk-grid-column-full, govuk-grid-row, govuk-heading-xl, govuk-input, govuk-label, govuk-label--m, govuk-link, govuk-select.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: None — this page is 100% inside the GOV.UK Design System toolbox and rebuilds cleanly with govuk-frontend macros. Note: the Country field is a native govuk-select over 254 options rather than accessible-autocomplete; the new app may want to upgrade it to an autocomplete for usability, but IPAFFS uses the plain select here.

fields.name: telephone
fields.label: Telephone number
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy (POLICY the rebuild may revisit) — Joi .empty('').required() in economic_operator.js:52-55 (TELEPHONE); max 30. The frontend Joi form REQUIRES telephone, but the Java model does NOT mark EconomicOperatorAddress.telephone/ukTelephone @NotNull for any group (EconomicOperatorAddress.java:44-50). So legacy client-side mandatoriness diverges from server-side model mandatoriness — the rebuild should decide deliberately whether telephone stays mandatory.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 90 (input type=tel, id=telephone); page object BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:31-33; value '01100100102' observed filled in trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 98 (confirmed observation). Requiredness resolved from legacy Joi validator.

fields.name: country
fields.label: Country
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — Joi .valid(...countries).empty('').required() in economic_operator.js:56-59 (COUNTRY), value validated against the live country-code list. Corroborated by the Java model: EconomicOperatorAddress.countryISOCode is @NotNull for the CVEDA group (EconomicOperatorAddress.java:40-42).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 90 (native govuk-select, 254 options incl. first 'Please select your country' placeholder + UK constituent nations England/Scotland/Wales/Northern Ireland). Page object selects it via getByLabel('Country') BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:35-37, confirming the label-for/id association. IMPORTANT: the tests select by OPTION VALUE, not display text — country.England = 'GB-ENG', country.Afghanistan = 'AF' (types/country.ts:238,2). So the select carries ISO-style coded values (GB-ENG etc.) behind the country-name labels. Legacy: topOption='Please select your country', items sourced from countriesSelectItems = countries.map({value:code, text:name}) (create_new_economic_operator.js:63; economic_operator_create.html:141-151). New app should source countries from a reference-data service keyed on these codes, not hardcode.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy (POLICY the rebuild may revisit) — Joi .lowercase().email({tlds:{allow:false}}).max(255).empty('').required() in economic_operator.js:44-48 (EMAIL). The frontend Joi form REQUIRES a validly-formatted email and lowercases it; the Java model does NOT mark EconomicOperatorAddress.email @NotNull for any group (EconomicOperatorAddress.java:52-53). Client-side mandatoriness diverges from the model — a deliberate rebuild decision.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 90 (input type=email, id=email); page object BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:39-41; value 'peakperformance@test.com' observed filled in trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 100 (confirmed observation). Requiredness resolved from legacy Joi validator.

validationMessages.trigger: value not in the valid country-code list
validationMessages.evidence: economic_operator.js:58 invalidMessage(labels.COUNTRY); invalidMessage = `${field} is not valid` (messages/en.js:19-21). Fires when the submitted code is not one of the live countries/regions codes (create_new_economic_operator.js:74 passes countryCodes to the validator).

discrepancies: Field label 'Branch address name' (rendered trace, confirmed) vs 'Branch name' (legacy template computes label as concat(toSentenceCase(addressBookType), ' name') = 'Branch name', economic_operator_create.html:57 + labels/en.js:73). The rendered page shows 'Branch address name' but the legacy source/validation label is 'Branch name' — the required-field error copy would therefore read 'Enter a branch name'. Kept the rendered 'Branch address name' as the display label; the divergence is a Reconcile conflict (IPAFFS source may lag what it serves, OR the trace ran a build where addressBookType differed).

Postcode field display label 'Postcode or ZIP code' (rendered trace, confirmed) vs validation label 'Post code' (legacy labels/en.js:81). The max-length error copy uses 'Post code' → 'Post code must be 32 characters or fewer'. Kept the rendered display label; recorded the validation-label divergence.

Telephone field display label 'Telephone number' (rendered trace, confirmed) vs validation label 'Telephone' (legacy labels/en.js:97). The required/max error copy uses 'Telephone' → 'Enter a telephone' / 'Telephone must be 30 characters or fewer'. Kept the rendered display label; recorded the validation-label divergence.

Client-side vs server-side mandatoriness divergence for telephone and email: the frontend Joi validator REQUIRES both (economic_operator.js:44-55), but the Java model marks neither EconomicOperatorAddress.telephone nor .email @NotNull for any validation group (EconomicOperatorAddress.java:44-53). The address model only requires addressLine1, city, countryISOCode, and only for the CVEDA (CHED-A) group — there is no CED/CHED-D @NotNull group on this address at all. Legacy per-page mandatoriness therefore comes entirely from the shared frontend form, not from a CHED-D model group.
openQuestions: MANDATORINESS NOW RESOLVED from the legacy frontend Joi validator (economic_operator.js): Branch address name, Address line 1, City or town, Telephone number, Country and Email address are all REQUIRED; Address line 2, Address line 3 and Postcode are OPTIONAL. This supersedes the earlier trace/test gap. Two of the required fields (Telephone, Email) are required only client-side — the Java model does not mark them @NotNull — so the rebuild should deliberately decide whether to keep them mandatory (see discrepancies).

This 'Add branch address' page is the SHARED economic-operator / address-book create form (economic_operator_create.html), reused across all trader types (consignor, consignee, importer, transporter, final-destination, permanent-address, charity, branch). It is NOT CHED-D-specific: the mandatoriness above is a property of the address-book create form and applies identically regardless of CHED type. Confirm the rebuild wants the same one-form-for-all-types approach or a CHED-D-specific branch-address form.

Country select uses coded option VALUES (GB-ENG for England, AF for Afghanistan — types/country.ts) behind display labels, sourced live from countriesSelectItems (create_new_economic_operator.js:63). The POST validates the submitted code against the live countries+regions code list (create_new_economic_operator.js:72-75). Confirm the new app's countries reference-data service exposes the same code set (and whether UK constituent nations England/Scotland/Wales/Northern Ireland must remain distinct selectable options rather than a single 'United Kingdom').

The address inputs carry HTML autocomplete tokens (company-name=organization, address-line-1/2/3=address-line1/2/3, city-or-town=address-level2, postcode=postal-code — economic_operator_create.html:52-129); worth mirroring in the rebuild for browser autofill.

Legacy field max-lengths (enforce in the rebuild): Branch name 255, Address line 1/2/3 255 each, City or town 58, Postcode 32, Telephone 30, Email 255 (economic_operator.js:22-59). Email is additionally lowercased and TLD-agnostically format-validated.

The legacy form also carries hidden crumb (CSRF) and etag (optimistic-concurrency, validated 16-18 chars) inputs, plus optional 'from-importer-review' and 'reimport' hidden flags and an approval-number field shown only for the public/commercial transporter type (isCommercialTransporterType) — none relevant to the branch-address instance but present in the shared template (economic_operator_create.html:25-26,40-50,192-197).

Journey behaviour (inferred, ched-d-workflows.ts:343-353): this page is reached via the Contact Address page's 'add a new branch address' link (ContactAddressPage.ts:7), and after Save and continue it routes to a Branch Address CONFIRMATION page (BranchAddressConfirmationPage). Legacy POST success redirects to `${IMPORTER_URL_PATH}/${type}/confirmation/${economicOperatorId}?fromCreate=true` (create_new_economic_operator.js:93). The rebuild must decide whether to keep this create -> confirm -> return -> re-save interstitial flow or collapse it.
